assertions.title: Review contract freezes and can only become stricter after first live execution

steps.action: Create work item with required attestation (human review, blocking=true)
steps.expected: Work item created with required_attestations field populated
steps.observed: Work item work_01KMH4H7CEQBQ0X4K1182J8P8W created with required_attestations=[{verifier_kind: human, method: review, blocking: true}], attestation_frozen_at=null

steps.action: Dispatch work item to trigger first live execution
steps.expected: Work transitions through claimed -> in_progress, and attestation_frozen_at gets set
steps.observed: Work transitioned claimed -> in_progress -> failed. attestation_frozen_at set to 2026-03-24T23:58:05.940085Z during in_progress state (frozen at job starting)

steps.action: Attempt to weaken contract via escalate_contract proposal (blocking: true -> blocking: false)
steps.expected: Proposal acceptance rejected with error indicating weakening is not allowed
steps.observed: Error: 'contract escalation must add stricter requirements, not weaken existing contract'

steps.action: Add stricter requirement via escalate_contract proposal (add new blocking attestation)
steps.expected: Proposal accepted, new requirement added with escalation audit fields
steps.observed: Proposal accepted (state=accepted). required_attestations now has 2 entries. New entry has escalated_at, escalation_by, escalation_reason fields set. Metadata includes contract_escalated_at and contract_escalation_proposal

steps.expected: Escalation metadata persists on work item and in proposals list
steps.observed: Work metadata has contract_escalated_at=2026-03-24T23:58:34Z, contract_escalation_proposal=wprop_01KMH4J8PRYZMJG4BTB00ZFXA9. Proposals list shows accepted escalation proposal with rationale.

assertions.title: Terminal success is gated by one frozen completion contract

steps.action: Create work item with required attestation (human review, blocking=true)
steps.expected: Work item created in ready state
steps.observed: Work item work_01KMH4JYGTS6JBA0D6GZZNJP1H created in ready state with required_attestations=[{verifier_kind: human, method: review, blocking: true}]

steps.action: Attempt to transition to done state without satisfying attestation requirements
steps.expected: Transition rejected with error about unresolved attestations
steps.observed: Error: 'work item work_01KMH4JYGTS6JBA0D6GZZNJP1H has unresolved required attestations'

steps.action: Submit attestation matching required slot (verifier_kind=human, method=review, result=passed)
steps.expected: Attestation recorded, work transitions to done with approval_state=pending
steps.observed: Attestation created. Work transitioned to execution_state=done, approval_state=pending

assertions.title: Code, docs, and persisted work-graph state share one explicit precedence rule

steps.action: Check README.md for explicit precedence rule
steps.expected: README contains explicit 'Contract Precedence' section stating runtime code is canonical
steps.observed: README has '### Contract Precedence' section clearly stating: 'When runtime code, committed documentation, and persisted work-graph state disagree, runtime code is the canonical source of truth'

steps.action: Verify precedence rule covers all three sources (code, docs, persisted state)
steps.expected: Rule explicitly mentions all three: runtime code, committed documentation, and persisted work-graph state
steps.observed: Rule explicitly lists: (1) Work execution states - internal/core/types.go, (2) CLI/API contracts - runtime implementation, (3) Attestation semantics - code's AttestationRecord structure

steps.action: Check that docs reference the precedence rule
steps.expected: Proposal/spec docs contain contract notes pointing to types.go and README
steps.observed: docs/spec-check-flow.md has contract note: 'The canonical work execution states are defined in internal/core/types.go... See the README for the precedence rule'. docs/review-simplify-worker-contract.md has same contract note.

steps.action: Verify CLI help text aligns with code definitions
steps.expected: CLI help for 'cogent work update --execution-state' lists valid states matching types.go
steps.observed: CLI help shows: 'Valid execution states: ready, claimed, in_progress, checking, blocked, done, failed, cancelled, archived' and notes 'awaiting_attestation is deprecated'. Code defines all these states including WorkExecutionStateAwaitingAttestation with comment 'deprecated alias for checking'

steps.expected: types.go defines all states, including deprecated ones with clear comments
steps.observed: internal/core/types.go defines WorkExecutionState constants with WorkExecutionStateAwaitingAttestation marked as 'deprecated alias for checking'

summary: Tested 3 assertions: all passed. VAL-CONTRACT-003 confirmed that attestation contracts freeze at first dispatch, weakening is blocked, and escalation creates audited stricter requirements. VAL-CONTRACT-004 confirmed that terminal success (done state) is gated by attestation requirements. VAL-CONTRACT-005 confirmed that the precedence rule is explicitly documented in README, referenced by proposal docs, and CLI help aligns with code definitions.
